Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "Victor Osei" or "revenue drag" or "delayed closings" (or similar) using search_emails to find Victor's earlier revenue impact email (subject: "Revenue impact snapshot on delayed closings") sent to robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com and grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, which estimates ~$15,000 total revenue drag on three delayed files using a one-week slip assumption described as "directional, not a formal forecast" and excluding lock extension costs.
OE 2: Search emails with a query related to "Premier Data" or "vendor" or "invoices" (or similar) using search_emails to find Victor's email to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com (subject: "Premier Data Solutions invoices") flagging eight monthly QuickBooks invoices at $600 each for Premier Data Solutions, all approved by Hana Kim, with no vendor onboarding packet, contract, statement of work, or purchase approval on file.
OE 3: Search Slack messages with a query related to "Premier Data" or "vendor" or "$600" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Victor's message in channel C001 (#general) alerting Grace about 8 straight months of $600 invoices to Premier Data Solutions in QuickBooks, all approved by Hana, with no vendor setup docs or contract found.
OE 4: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria filtering for "Premier Data" or vendor name "Premier Data Solutions" (or similar) to find the Premier Data Solutions bill (vendor id 143, DocNumber PDS-030126, TotalAmt $600, TxnDate 2026-03-01, line description "Monthly data services"). Only one bill appears in QuickBooks despite Victor's email and Slack message referencing eight months of $600 payments this gap between claimed payment history (8 x $600 = $4,800) and the single bill on record ($600) is itself notable and should inform the cost exposure summary.
OE 5: Search QuickBooks vendors using quickbooks_search_vendors with criteria filtering for "Premier Data" (or similar) to retrieve the Premier Data Solutions vendor record (id 143), confirming the billing address is a PMB (private mailbox) at "4216 Park Rd PMB 118, Charlotte, NC 28209" which supports the suspicious nature of the vendor flagged by Victor.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria filtering for "lock extension" or "LOCK-EXT" or "Mercer" or "00184" (or similar) to find the three lock extension bills tied to loan LN-2026-00184 (Evan Mercer): (1) DocNumber LOCK-EXT-00184, $375, TxnDate 2026-03-20, vendor "Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations" with an empty Line array; (2) DocNumber LOCKEXT-00184-1, $375, TxnDate 2026-04-02, vendor "Keystone Mortgage Partners Operating", description "1st lock extension fee for Evan Mercer loan after build delay"; (3) DocNumber PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER, $375, TxnDate 2026-04-16, description "Second lock extension fee held pending cost ownership for Mercer loan". All QB bills are $375; no $500 bill exists.
OE 7: Search Stripe charges with a query related to "lock extension" or "Mercer" or "LN-2026-00184" (or similar) using stripe_search_charges to find the three borrower-side lock extension charges: (1) ch_scenario_c66_lockext1, $375.00, "Rate lock extension Mercer (LN-2026-00184)"; (2) ch_scenario_c66_lockext2, $375.00, "Rate lock extension (2nd) Mercer (LN-2026-00184)"; (3) ch_scenario_c66_lockext3, $500.00, "Rate lock extension (3rd) Mercer (LN-2026-00184)." The Stripe total is $1,250 ($375 + $375 + $500), while the QuickBooks total is $1,125 (3 x $375). The $500 third extension charge in Stripe has no matching $500 bill in QuickBooks this is the key mismatch Victor referenced.
OE 8: Search mortgage LOS for loan LN-2026-00184 using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "LN-2026-00184" or "Mercer" or similar) or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00184") to retrieve the loan record confirming: new construction purchase, $340,000 loan amount, $425,000 purchase price, rate 6.875%, rate lock expiration 2026-03-23, status "processing," closing_date null (not yet closed), and notes "New construction purchase. Build delays causing rate lock extensions."
OE 9: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria filtering for "revenue impact" or "REV-IMPACT" (or similar) to find the internal revenue impact invoice (DocNumber REV-IMPACT-0318, Id 9731, TxnDate 2026-03-18, TotalAmt $15,000, customer "Internal Revenue Impact Review") with line description "Estimated commission/revenue drag tied to three delayed closings versus expected on-time funding cadence." This is the formal QuickBooks record of Victor's ~$15K estimate referenced in the prompt.
OE 10: Look up Robert Calloway's and Grace Yamamoto's email addresses using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Robert Calloway" or similar) and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Grace Yamamoto" or similar) to confirm r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com and grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com as the recipients for the cost exposure summary email. Note: Robert's contacts record uses r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, though robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com also appears in some email threads in the universe.
OE 11: Look up Hana Kim's contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Hana Kim" or similar) to confirm hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com and her Slack user identity for the notification message.
OE 12: Send an email using send_email (sender: victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com, recipients: [r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com]) with a written cost exposure summary covering: (a) the lock extension charge reconciliation for LN-2026-00184 showing the Stripe vs QuickBooks mismatch ($1,250 Stripe total vs $1,125 in QB, with the $500 third extension unmatched); (b) the updated revenue drag analysis on the three delayed closings, reconciled against actual billing and loan records rather than Victor's earlier directional ~$15K estimate; (c) the Premier Data Solutions vendor payment exposure Victor's email and Slack claimed 8 months x $600 = $4,800 but only one $600 bill (DocNumber PDS-030126) exists in QuickBooks, with no supporting contract or vendor setup documentation and a PMB mailing address.
OE 13: Send a Slack message notifying Hana Kim using conversations_add_message to let her know that Victor is pulling the numbers on both the lock extension charges and the Premier Data vendor payments before Robert's meeting, so she is aware the financial data is being reviewed. The message may be sent via a public channel (e.g., C001 #general or C002 #loan-processing) with an @mention of Hana, since no DM channel for Hana Kim exists in the universe. Hana's Slack user ID is keystone_d5f0a921b139.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

2 The email body (visible in send_email parameters) sent to Robert and Grace references the Stripe vs QuickBooks mismatch on lock extension charges for loan LN-2026-00184 (Evan Mercer), identifying that the Stripe total is $1,250 while the QuickBooks total is $1,125.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly identifies the Stripe vs QB discrepancy: 'Stripe collected $1,625 but QB only shows $1,500 in bills' and flags the $125 net gap. The criterion's alternative evidence conditions are satisfied.
✓Run #2The email body explicitly states 'Stripe collected $1,625 vs. QB bills of $1,500 = $125 gap. The 3rd Stripe charge was $500 but there is no QB bill at that amount.' The criterion allows for identifying the $125 gap or noting the $500 third Stripe charge has no matching QB bill — both are present in the email.
✓Run #3The email body explicitly references both figures: 'Total collected via Stripe: $1,625.00' (including the flagged duplicate charge) and 'Total billed in QB: $1,125.00', and identifies a '$500 gap -- The 3rd Stripe extension ($500) has NO corresponding QuickBooks bill.' The criterion allows for identifying the $500 gap or noting the unmatched $500 Stripe charge, which was done.
✓Run #4The email body explicitly identifies the $125 gap ('Stripe collected $1,625 but QB only records $1,500') and specifically calls out that 'the 3rd Stripe charge ($500) has no matching QB bill.' This satisfies the criterion's alternative condition of identifying the $125 gap or the unmatched $500 Stripe charge.
✓Run #5The email body explicitly lists a Stripe total of $1,625 (4 charges) vs QB total of $1,125 across 3 bills. While the Stripe figure is $1,625 (including a possible duplicate), the email clearly identifies the three core Stripe charges ($375+$375+$500=$1,250) and the QB total of $1,125, explicitly noting the $500 third charge has no QB bill. The discrepancy between systems is clearly articulated with specific dollar amounts.
✓Run #6The email body explicitly identifies Stripe total ($1,625 across 4 charges) vs QuickBooks total ($1,125 across 3 bills), and specifically notes the $500 third Stripe charge has no matching QB bill. While the Stripe total is $1,625 (not $1,250) because the agent found a 4th charge, the evidence criterion is satisfied via the explicit note that the $500 third Stripe charge has no matching QB bill, which is the core mismatch.
3 The email body (visible in send_email parameters) sent to Robert and Grace identifies the $500 third lock extension charge in Stripe as having no matching $500 bill in QuickBooks.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly states: 'The 3rd extension ($500 in Stripe) has no matching QB bill at all,' and all listed QB bills are $375 each, making the $500 unmatched charge clearly identified.
✓Run #2The email body explicitly states: 'The 3rd Stripe charge was $500 but there is no QB bill at that amount — suggests the 3rd extension was either not billed to the lender or was recorded at the wrong amount.' This directly identifies the unmatched $500 Stripe charge.
✓Run #3The email body explicitly states under DISCREPANCIES: '(a) $500 gap -- The 3rd Stripe extension ($500) has NO corresponding QuickBooks bill.' This directly satisfies the criterion.
✓Run #4The email body explicitly states: 'the 3rd Stripe charge ($500) has no matching QB bill.' This directly identifies the $500 third Stripe charge as unmatched in QuickBooks.
✓Run #5The email body explicitly states 'The 3rd extension charged in Stripe ($500) has no corresponding QB bill at all.' and lists it in the Stripe breakdown as '3rd extension: $500.00' while noting this charge is absent from the QB records. This directly satisfies the criterion.
✓Run #6The email body explicitly states: 'The 3rd lock extension ($500.00) charged in Stripe has NO matching bill in QuickBooks.' This directly satisfies the criterion.
4 The email body (visible in send_email parameters) sent to Robert and Grace references the Premier Data Solutions vendor payment exposure, noting that only one $600 bill (DocNumber PDS-030126) exists in QuickBooks despite claims of 8 months of payments.
outcome
 ✗✓✗✓✓✓ 67%
✗Run #1The email asserts '$4,800 paid out (8 months x $600)' without noting that only one bill (PDS-030126 / B-2108) was actually found in QuickBooks. The QB search confirmed only one PDS bill, but the email does not surface this discrepancy between the claimed 8-month history and the single bill on record.
✓Run #2The email body explicitly states '8 consecutive months of $600 invoices, all approved by Hana Kim, totaling an estimated $4,800. QB currently shows 1 confirmed bill: B-2108, $600, paid, dated 3/1/2026, doc# PDS-030126.' This directly identifies the gap between claimed 8-month history and a single bill on record.
